HEALTH X PLATFORM : BOARD APPROVES COMPOSITE DEMERGER & MERGER SCHEME 🔄🏥💰

• 💰 Financial Services business of Health X will be demerged into Microsec Resources, creating a separate listed financial services entity

• 🏛️ Resulting company (Microsec Resources) will seek listing on NSE and BSE after necessary approvals

🚀 SpaceX Connection: 

A Hidden Gem in INOX India's Story

The investment world gets excited when a company wins a contract from a global giant. But when that giant is Elon Musk's 🇺🇸SpaceX, investors should pay even closer attention.

SpaceX is one of the most technologically advanced aerospace companies in the world, with an estimated valuation running into tens of billions of dollars. Its standards for suppliers are exceptionally high, as even a small failure can have massive consequences in space missions.

🇮🇳 INOX India: One of the Few Indian Suppliers
INOX India recently received orders from SpaceX for large-sized cryogenic tanks, building upon the successful execution of earlier orders.
This is important because repeat orders are rarely awarded unless the customer is satisfied with the quality, reliability, and execution capabilities of the supplier.

Why This Matters
Cryogenic storage is at the heart of modern space exploration. Rockets require storage and handling of extremely low-temperature fuels, and this demands world-class engineering expertise.

By supplying such equipment to SpaceX, INOX India has effectively demonstrated that its technology meets some of the toughest engineering standards globally.

More Than Just an LNG Company
Many investors still associate INOX India primarily with LNG infrastructure and industrial gas equipment.
However, its growing presence in:
Aerospace
Space technology
Scientific research
Advanced cryogenic applications
shows that the company is quietly expanding into highly specialized, high value sectors.

The Real Takeaway
The significance is not merely the size of the order.
The real significance is the identity of the customer.
When one of the world's most advanced aerospace companies chooses an Indian supplier and then comes back with additional orders, it serves as a powerful endorsement of that supplier's technological capabilities.

In a market filled with companies claiming global ambitions, INOX India is one of the few Indian engineering companies that can say it has earned the trust of SpaceX. 🚀🇮🇳

Emerald Finance Limited has onboarded Yerik International Private Limited, Delhi as its newest employer partner under its Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program.

About the Program:
Employees can access a portion of their earned salary at any point during the month through a fully structured salary-linked advance model - disbursed in close coordination with the employer and recovered seamlessly through monthly payroll deductions.

Employee Well-Being:
The program is designed to address short-term liquidity needs of employees without disrupting their monthly financial planning. By eliminating dependency on the traditional fixed pay cycle, it empowers employees with greater control over their finances and promotes overall financial well-being at the workplace.

Repayment Structure:
Repayments are structured through automatic payroll deductions at month end - ensuring a disciplined, transparent and completely stress-free credit experience for every employee enrolled under the program.

This partnership furthers Emerald Finance's mission of making earned wage access a standard employee benefit across India - structured, responsible and built for the modern workforce.

Jefferies On Metals

JSW Steel/TATA  Steel trading above their long-term average EV/EBITDA

High entry valuation is a key investor concern.

Last 15-year data shows that 12-month stock returns were 15-39ppt higher when entry EV/EBITDA was above long-term average vs below.

This inverse correlation arises as multiples expand in periods of depressed spreads, with anticipation of earning upgrades.

FY27-28 EPS for JSTL/TATA are 13-36% above street.

JSW Steel is top buy in metals.

Auto Index has moved 3x since COVID and in a general sense one would think that most of the auto ancillaries would have grown faster than the OEMs themselves...

The answer is both yes and no. Not all, but a few grew fast and they earned that auto ancillary premium. Interestingly, there were a few common patterns across each one of them.

Electricals compounded at 17%. Batteries at 8% (yes, the EV transition is not enough to save the day). Same sector label, completely different businesses.

After spending years tracking this space, here's the framework I'd use to separate the compounders from the capital traps:

1. Count the growth levers, not the growth rate

Companies pulling multiple levers simultaneously with new products, new geographies, new customers, acquisitions outperformed in every single cycle. Why? Because that's where the visibility on diversification of the revenue base comes from.

2. Not all content growth is equal

The most powerful dynamic of the decade was rising content per vehicle. But content growth has two very different flavours:

> Regulatory content (BS6, airbags, ABS, TPMS) - one-time. Vehicles comply, content is sold, growth rate normalises.

→ Structural content (premiumisation, EV adoption, connected car, digital clusters) - this compounds. It's driven by consumer preference, not a compliance deadline.

For the next 3–4 years, the question isn't "will auto volumes grow?" — it's "which companies are positioned on content categories that didn't even exist in FY16?"

3. The valuation anomaly most people are ignoring

Not all segments deserve the same multiple. Body & Glass is delivering 30% PAT CAGR. It will command a premium — why? Longevity of earnings irrespective of any sort of engine transition. Be it EV or ICE, both need body and glass.

And if you're paying a premium for a story that may have missed in the past or carries any sort of transition risk, then one has to be completely aware of that. At those multiples, even in-line delivery is arithmetically a de-rating quarter.

Bottom line:

The sector's balance sheet is the healthiest in a decade with Net Debt/EBITDA at 0.18x. The consensus FY26–28 PAT CAGR is 21%.

This sector rewards the diversified over the concentrated, the structural content grower over the compliance beneficiary, and the consistent deliverer over the compelling storyteller.

One has to find a business bottoms-up in this sector that has such characteristics one that has created a competitive moat, is diversifying revenue streams (organically or inorganically), and where valuations can be argued upon.

Source - Equirus

**India's Economic & Infrastructure Transformation: Comparing 2014 vs 2026 🇮🇳**

Jefferies report interesting read. Basically saying one thing clearly: AI data center demand is not the problem. Physical supply is the bottleneck. The world wants far more data center capacity than the ecosystem can build and commission fast enough. The cleanest theme: AI infrastructure bottlenecks and their beneficiaries. The best names are the ones sitting at real choke points where demand is exploding but supply cannot scale easily. Key buckets: Cooling:
[$VRT](https://x.com/search?q=%24VRT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$CARR](https://x.com/search?q=%24CARR&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$TT](https://x.com/search?q=%24TT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$JCI](https://x.com/search?q=%24JCI&src=cashtag_click)
Power equipment / transformers / grid:
[$GEV](https://x.com/search?q=%24GEV&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$ETN](https://x.com/search?q=%24ETN&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$WCC](https://x.com/search?q=%24WCC&src=cashtag_click)
EPC / construction:
[$PRIM](https://x.com/search?q=%24PRIM&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$MTZ](https://x.com/search?q=%24MTZ&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$PWR](https://x.com/search?q=%24PWR&src=cashtag_click)
Data center capacity owners / developers:
[$DLR](https://x.com/search?q=%24DLR&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$EQIX](https://x.com/search?q=%24EQIX&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$CORZ](https://x.com/search?q=%24CORZ&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$WULF](https://x.com/search?q=%24WULF&src=cashtag_click)
Hyperscalers:
[$AMZN](https://x.com/search?q=%24AMZN&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$MSFT](https://x.com/search?q=%24MSFT&src=cashtag_click)
,
[$GOOGL](https://x.com/search?q=%24GOOGL&src=cashtag_click)
The big takeaway: The next layer is physical infrastructure: power, cooling, land, construction, grid, and capacity. That is where the real bottleneck sits.

Zee Eyes Gold Rush with Big Ad Rate Push; Official FIFA World Cup 2026 Broadcaster Pitches ₹16,600 Per 10 Seconds Unified Rate — 10% Above 2022 Edition

The Ad Rate — What Zee Is Offering
Zee Entertainment — official broadcaster of the 2026 FIFA World Cup — offering a unified ad rate of ~₹16,600 per 10 seconds combining TV and digital

Rate is an increase of little over 10% over the 2022 edition price

Reliance-backed Viacom18 (now part of JioStar) had charged ~₹15,000 unified ad rate during the last World Cup four years ago

For comparison, Premier League — which has a sizeable viewer base in India — draws ad rates of ~₹10,000 per 10 seconds

RBI's Hidden Liquidity Play

ECB and FCCB flows plunged 30% in FY26 to $42.9B. The RBI is keeping repo rates steady at 5.25% while managing supply shocks, forcing a structural pivot toward alternative dollar mobilization.

The consensus treats this status quo as a pause, missing the liquidity trap. By absorbing hedging costs for FCNR-B deposits, the central bank is aggressively subsidizing domestic deposit growth

This regulatory alpha triggers a regime shift. Swapping external debt for domestic credit transmission protects corporate margins from overseas de-rating, even as CPI inflation peaks at 5.9%.

Bottom Line: Stop watching the repo rate headline. The real alpha is in the backend forex reforms designed to cap domestic yields and guarantee credit growth without bleeding the currency.
